changes: Added explicit temporal verification rule: distinguish between confirmed events vs projections when researching 2026 developments
Reduced recommended search count from 4-6 to 3-5 searches to prevent information overload
reasoning: The primary accuracy issue stems from temporal confusion - presenting 2026 events as historical when current date is March 2, 2026. Adding explicit temporal verification rules should prevent this. Reducing search count addresses the pattern where 10-12 searches correlate with lower performance vs 5-7 searches achieving higher scores. These targeted changes preserve the strong intellectual honesty approach while fixing the main accuracy failure mode.
